text:
There once was a young man named Tom. He lived in a small town and was a diligent student, excelling in everything from math to science to literature. Tom had many friends—and an even more diverse collection of family members—who admired his intelligence and ambition. 

One day, Tom heard about a prestigious engineering college far away in another state. Intrigued by the challenge and longing for a new adventure, he decided to apply. He poured his soul into his work, complete with all the necessary essays, letters of recommendation, and even a letter of intent that spoke volumes about his determination and passion for his chosen field.

After months of waiting, one day an unexpected letter arrived. It wasn't from the admissions committee; it was from his grandma, who, sensing his excitement, had written on his behalf. To his surprise and joy, Tom saw she had not only secured him a spot but also recommended him to the prestigious faculty for his stellar academic achievements. His heart swelled with gratitude and
